notes: Selected after local probing because it exposes machine-readable JSON events with session IDs and token counts.
The local crush install advertised --yolo in help output but rejected the flag at runtime, which makes it a poor default for unattended benchmarking here.
The explicit build agent has permissive filesystem/tool rules, which is the closest match to the requested autonomous coding workflow.

models.label: Gemma 4 31B
models.selection_reason: Hosted locally via llama-swap. Requires llama.cpp b8665+ for the dedicated Gemma 4 tool call parser (PR #21418). Skipped by default; re-enable for llama-swap benchmark runs.

models.label: GLM 4.7 Flash BF16
models.selection_reason: Hosted locally via llama-swap. Needs --jinja --reasoning-format none to suppress <think> tags in content. Tool calling works correctly with these flags.

models.label: Llama 4 Scout
models.selection_reason: Hosted locally via llama-swap. Skipped by default: llama.cpp has no parser for Llama 4's pythonic tool call format — model outputs tool calls as plain text content instead of structured tool_calls. Requires upstream llama.cpp support (similar to vLLM's llama4_pythonic parser).

models.label: Qwen 3 32B
models.selection_reason: Requested local model family; exact hosted variant available through opencode. Superseded by the Qwen 3.5 line for future local benchmarking. Skipped by default after benchmark preview averaged 7.96 output tok/s over the first 3 steps (< 20.00).

models.label: Qwen 3 Coder Next
models.selection_reason: Best direct coding-oriented local Qwen variant matching the original benchmark brief. Skipped by default after benchmark preview measured 6.59 output tok/s (< 20.00).

models.label: Qwen 3.5 35B
models.selection_reason: Requested local model family; exact hosted variant available through opencode. Skipped by default because the current benchmark pass is restricted to OpenRouter models that previously completed successfully.

models.label: Qwen 3.6 35B
models.selection_reason: Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B (released 2026-04-15). Same qwen3_5_moe architecture as 3.5 (35B total / 3B active MoE). Q3_K_M ~16 GB — drop-in replacement for 3.5. Significant benchmark gains: SWE-bench 73.4 (was 70), Terminal-Bench 51.5 (was 40.5), MCPMark 37 (was 27). Has vision encoder. Same chat template and llama.cpp flags as 3.5.

models.label: Qwen 3.5 122B
models.selection_reason: Hosted locally via llama-swap. Needs --reasoning-format none on llama-server to avoid reasoning_content tokens that some clients mishandle. Tool calling works correctly with Qwen chat template.

models.label: GPT OSS 20B
models.selection_reason: Added as a local Ollama GPT OSS baseline for later warmup and benchmark testing.

models.label: Nemotron Cascade 2
models.selection_reason: Added as a local Ollama Nemotron Cascade 2 candidate for later warmup and benchmark testing. This entry uses explicit local model metadata because it is not yet mapped in the home opencode config.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.6
models.selection_reason: Exact requested cloud model.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.7
models.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 on OpenRouter. Built for long-running async agents. Same pricing as 4.6: $5/M input, $25/M output. 1M context, 128K max output. Released 2026-04-16.

models.label: opencode Opus 4.7 + GLM 5.1 coder

opencode_subagent.name: coder
opencode_subagent.description: Use proactively for concrete coding execution: file edits, refactors, bug fixes, test writing, boilerplate generation, single-file implementations. The primary (Opus) plans and delegates; this agent (GLM 5.1) executes cheaply. Skip delegation for cross-file architectural decisions or debugging with unclear root cause.
opencode_subagent.prompt: You are a focused coding agent. The parent (Opus) has already decided the approach — your job is to execute cleanly. Follow instructions precisely. Don't re-plan or second-guess. Match existing codebase style. Keep changes minimal — do what was asked, nothing more. Default to no comments.

models.selection_reason: opencode multi-agent: Opus 4.7 primary + GLM 5.1 (Z.ai) coding subagent. Tests whether the cost-effective Chinese model handles coding when Opus plans. GLM 5.1 via Z.ai coding plan endpoint (subscription). Comparable to Claude Code's opus+sonnet variant but with a non-Anthropic coder.

models.label: opencode Opus 4.7 + Qwen 3.6 local coder

opencode_subagent.name: coder
opencode_subagent.description: Use proactively for concrete coding execution: file edits, refactors, simple implementations. The primary (Opus) plans and delegates; this agent (local Qwen 3.6 via llama-swap) executes locally to save API cost. Skip delegation for complex reasoning or gem-specific API work where cloud models are more reliable.
opencode_subagent.prompt: You are a local coding agent running on llama-swap. The parent (Opus) has decided the approach — just execute. Follow instructions precisely. Match existing codebase style. Keep changes minimal.

models.selection_reason: opencode multi-agent: Opus 4.7 primary (cloud) + Qwen 3.6 35B (local llama-swap) coding subagent. Tests the 'local hybrid' hypothesis — expensive cloud orchestrator with free local executor. Depends on llama-swap running with qwen3.6:35b loaded.

models.label: GPT 5.4 Pro
models.selection_reason: Chosen from the OpenRouter GPT 5.4 family as the largest and most coding-oriented variant. Skipped by default because it failed in the previous benchmark pass.

models.label: GPT 5.4 xHigh (Codex)
models.selection_reason: GPT 5.4 via Codex CLI at xhigh reasoning effort. Tier 2: correct entry point (RubyLLM.chat + ask + response.content) but add_message uses keyword args instead of positional hash — crashes on multi-turn. ~$16/run (15x Claude). Polished architecture but wrong API calling convention.

models.label: GPT 5.4 xHigh + medium coder (Codex multi-agent)

codex_subagent.name: coder
codex_subagent.description: Use proactively for concrete coding execution: file edits, refactors, bug fixes, test writing, boilerplate generation. The main session (xhigh) plans and delegates; this agent (medium) executes. Skip delegation for cross-file architectural decisions or complex root-cause debugging.
codex_subagent.prompt: You are a focused coding agent. The parent (xhigh) has already decided the approach — your job is to execute cleanly. Follow instructions precisely. Don't re-plan or second-guess. Match existing codebase style. Keep changes minimal — do what was asked, nothing more. Default to no comments. Run tests after significant edits if the tooling is obvious.

models.selection_reason: Codex multi-agent: xhigh plans and orchestrates, medium/balanced handles coding. Tests whether GPT 5.4 at lower effort can execute well when the xhigh parent makes decisions. Comparison against gpt_5_4_codex (xhigh alone).

models.label: GPT 5.4 xHigh + low coder (Codex multi-agent)

codex_subagent.name: coder
codex_subagent.description: Use proactively for simple, high-volume coding execution: file edits, boilerplate generation, test scaffolding, single-file implementations. The main session (xhigh) plans; this agent (low) executes as cheaply as possible. Skip delegation for anything requiring judgment.
codex_subagent.prompt: You are a fast coding agent. The parent has already decided what to do — just execute. Follow instructions literally. Keep changes minimal. No comments unless asked.

models.selection_reason: Codex multi-agent: xhigh plans, low handles fast coding. Tests the 'cheap executor' hypothesis — whether minimal reasoning on the subagent is enough when the parent provides the plan.

models.label: Kimi K2.5
models.selection_reason: Chosen as the latest/highest Kimi variant listed by OpenRouter locally.

models.label: Kimi K2.6
models.selection_reason: Direct successor to K2.5. $0.74/$4.66 per M, 256K context, tool calling supported. Tests whether K2.6 fixes K2.5's Tier 3 hallucinations of RubyLLM add_message() and complete().

models.label: Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro
models.selection_reason: Xiaomi's flagship coding model. $1/$3 per M, 1M context, tool calling supported. Brand-new family we haven't tested — competitive pricing with mid-tier Chinese models like Kimi and GLM.

models.label: GLM 5
models.selection_reason: Chosen as the latest/highest GLM variant listed by OpenRouter locally; this replaces the local GLM test. Skipped by default because it completed with errors in the previous benchmark pass.

models.label: Qwen 3.6 Plus
models.selection_reason: Added from OpenRouter cloud availability; chose the non-preview Qwen 3.6 Plus variant exposed locally. Skipped by default because it completed with errors in the previous benchmark pass.

models.label: Qwen 3.5 397B Cloud
models.selection_reason: Added as the OpenRouter cloud Qwen 3.5 flagship under the requested qwen3.5:397b-cloud benchmark slot. Skipped by default because it stalled after completing validation steps and never emitted a terminal stop.

models.label: Gemma 4 31B Cloud
models.selection_reason: Google Gemma 4 31B IT BF16 served via Ollama's hosted cloud (https://ollama.com). Originally added to bypass the local llama.cpp parser bugs that caused infinite repetition loops on local Q3/Q8 GGUFs. Curl tests confirm the model itself works correctly for tool calling. **However, opencode benchmark runs hit HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout consistently around 20-24K total tokens of conversation history** — Cloudflare edge appears to enforce a ~100s per-request limit which 20K+ token prefill exceeds. Tried maxRetries:5 (didn't help — failures are consistent, not transient). Set limit.context:16384 to force opencode history trimming below the wall. Skipped by default until either Ollama Cloud raises the timeout or we test via Google's native Gemini API. Requires OLLAMA_API_KEY env var with Ollama Cloud subscription.

models.label: Llama 4 Scout Cloud
models.selection_reason: Added as the OpenRouter cloud Llama 4 Scout benchmark counterpart to the unusable local Scout path. Skipped by default because it does not currently resolve cleanly in this opencode build.

models.label: Nemotron 3 Super Cloud
models.selection_reason: Added as the closest OpenRouter cloud Nemotron line available after local Nemotron Cascade 2 proved unusable in this harness. Skipped by default because it still needs a clean first benchmark run.

models.label: MiniMax M2.7
models.selection_reason: Chosen as the largest/latest MiniMax variant listed by OpenRouter locally.

models.label: DeepSeek V3.2
models.selection_reason: Latest DeepSeek model on OpenRouter. Input $0.26/M, output $0.38/M.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Flash
models.selection_reason: DeepSeek V4 Flash — budget-tier variant at $0.14/M input, $0.28/M output (cheaper than V3.2). 1M context. Tool calling supported via OpenRouter. Test whether V4 fixes the RubyLLM API hallucination that made V3.2 Tier 3. Phase 2 disabled: DeepSeek's thinking-mode API rejects replayed `reasoning_content` tokens from opencode's session continuation.

models.label: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.selection_reason: DeepSeek V4 Pro — premium variant at $1.74/M input, $3.48/M output. 1M context. Uses thinking mode by default which requires the client to echo reasoning_content on subsequent turns (opencode doesn't). reasoning=false tells opencode to treat it as a non-reasoning model so it won't extract/pass back reasoning_content.

models.label: Step 3.5 Flash
models.selection_reason: StepFun Step 3.5 Flash on OpenRouter. Input $0.10/M, output $0.30/M.

models.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 on OpenRouter. Input $3.00/M, output $15.00/M.

models.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.selection_reason: Latest Google Gemini model with enhanced SWE performance and agentic reliability. Input $2.00/M, output $12.00/M.

models.label: Grok 4.20
models.selection_reason: xAI's latest flagship on OpenRouter. Fastest model in the benchmark (8 min) but produced architecturally broken code: bypassed RubyLLM with ruby-openai (only in dev/test group, NameError in prod), used format.turbo_stream without installing turbo-rails, RUBY_VERSION=4.0.2 Dockerfile bug. Tier 3 — broken core.

models.label: GLM 5.1
models.selection_reason: Z.ai's latest flagship GLM model. Uses Z.ai coding plan endpoint at https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4 (NOT the general /api/paas/v4) — Lite subscription includes glm-5.1 only via the coding endpoint. Completed in 22 min with 24 tests, correct primary RubyLLM.chat/ask usage, but invented chat.user/chat.assistant for multi-turn history seeding (single-turn works, multi-turn crashes). Tier 2 — works with caveats.

models.label: Qwen 3.5 27B Claude Distilled
models.selection_reason: Qwen 3.5 27B distilled from Claude 4.6 Opus reasoning traces (Jackrong/Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled). Tests whether Claude reasoning distillation transfers RubyLLM API correctness — most non-Anthropic models hallucinate the gem's API, so a Claude-distilled Qwen is an interesting natural experiment.

models.label: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B
models.selection_reason: Most popular dedicated coder of the Qwen 2.5 generation. Sourced from Ollama (Q4_K_M ~19 GB). On NVIDIA 5090 fits with 64K context.

models.label: Qwen 3 Coder 30B
models.selection_reason: Qwen 3 dedicated coder variant (the regular 30B, not the 51 GB qwen3-coder-next-ctx). Direct comparison with the general qwen3:32b. Sourced from Ollama (Q4_K_M ~18 GB).

models.label: Qwen 3.5 27B Sushi Coder RL
models.selection_reason: Qwen 3.5 27B fine-tuned via reinforcement learning on Codeforces problems (bigatuna/Qwen3.5-27b-Sushi-Coder-RL). Q4_K_M ~15 GB. Tests whether RL coding fine-tuning transfers correct RubyLLM API usage — direct comparison with the Claude reasoning distillation (qwen3.5:27b-claude) and the general qwen3.5:35b.

models.label: Gemma 4 31B (Ollama Cloud)
models.selection_reason: Google Gemma 4 31B IT BF16 served via Ollama's hosted cloud (https://ollama.com). Bypasses the local llama.cpp parser bugs that caused infinite repetition loops on local Q3/Q8 GGUFs. Tests whether Gemma 4 is actually capable for agentic tool calling when served by Google's full-precision infrastructure rather than crippled by quantization + parser regressions. Requires OLLAMA_API_KEY env var with an Ollama Cloud subscription.
